person.name: Maud Downes

memoryArtifacts.headline: Mobberley Church, view of tower, in Mobberley England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Maud, widow of William Mobberley, afterwards married John Dumbill Sr.

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Elizabeth, married Hugh Venables.

2.) Emma, married Robert Grosvenor.

3.) Margery, married Richard Bold.

4.) Mary, married Nicholas Leicester.

5.) Cecily, married John Domville.

6.) Joan, married William Atherton.

zh.Pott Shrigley, Cheshire, England: 英國爾波特 史利格利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Downes於大約1280年出生在英國爾霍利時，她的父親Robert de Downes43歲，母親Margaret Fitton11278歲。她和William Mobberly至少有2個兒子和7個女兒。她去世於1348年之後在英國爾。

zh-hans.Pott Shrigley, Cheshire, England: 英国爾波特 史利格利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Downes于大约1280年出生在英国爾霍利时，她的父亲Robert de Downes43岁，母亲Margaret Fitton11278岁。她和William Mobberly至少有2个儿子和7个女儿。她去世于1348年之后在英国爾。

nameMeanings.name: Downes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of Down with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (counties Clare and Limerick): shortened Anglicized form of Irish Gaelic Ó Dubháin (see Doane ).

variants.name: Down
meanings: English (Devon): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a down or low hill, from Middle English dun(e), doun(e) (Old English dūn) ‘down, low hill’. See also Downer .

English (Devon): possibly in some cases a patronymic from the Old English personal name Dūn, but later names of this origin cannot reliably be distinguished from those originating from the topographic sense. See Dunn 2.

variants.name: Downs
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Downes  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Downes was born about 1280, in Chorley, Cheshire, England, her father, Robert de Downes, was 43 and her mother, Margaret Fitton, was 11278. She had at least 2 sons and 7 daughters with William Mobberly. She died after 1348, in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Downes est née environ 1280, à Chorley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Downes, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Margaret Fitton, avait 11278 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles avec William Mobberly. Elle est décédée après 1348, à Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Downes um 1280 in Chorley, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Downes 43 und ihre Mutter Margaret Fitton 11278. Sie hatte mit William Mobberly mindestens 2 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1348 in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Downes è nata il circa 1280 nel Chorley, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Robert de Downes, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Margaret Fitton, aveva 11278 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 7 figlie con William Mobberly. Morì dopo il 1348, nel Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Downesが約1280年にイギリスチェシュアイアチオルリーで生まれた時，父Robert de Downesは43歳，母Margaret Fittonは11278歳であった。彼女はWilliam Mobberlyとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1348年以後にイギリスチェシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Downes(이)가 대략 1280년 영국 체실르 초를리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Downes(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Fitton(은)는 11278세였다. 그녀는 William Mobberly(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 이후 영국 체실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Downes nasceu aproximadamente 1280, em Chorley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Downes, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Fitton, tinha 11278 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas com William Mobberly. Ela faleceu após 1348, em Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Downes родилась около 1280 г. в местности чаурли, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Downes было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Fitton, 11278. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей от William Mobberly. Она умерла после 1348 г. в местности чешир, Англия.
